testModifications.reasoning: No test modifications were made by implementer. The outbox-fan-out-registry.spec.ts tests are all passing.

criteria.criterion: AC1: OutboxFanOutRegistry.register(eventType, handler) adds a handler and it is called on subsequent dispatch() calls for that eventType
AC2: Calling register() for the same eventType twice adds both handlers instead of replacing the first
AC3: On app startup, all handlers from DEFAULT_HANDLERS are registered via OutboxFanOutRegistry.register()
AC4: When dispatch() is called with eventType='ticket_event', all handlers for ticket_event are executed sequentially in registration order
AC5: If a handler throws, the error is caught and logged and the next handler still runs
AC6: The document_indexed event payload includes { sourceId, content, metadata }, sufficient for future LexicalIndex.addDocument() handling
AC7: The graphify_import event payload includes { projectId, nodeCount, linkCount }, sufficient for future graph handlers
AC8: Handler registration is testable by registering a mock handler and asserting it is called when dispatch() fires

reasoning: All 11 story-scoped tests pass. Implementation is clean and follows NestJS conventions: uses OnModuleInit for startup registration, proper TypeScript typing, Logger for error logging, and sequential handler execution with error isolation. DEFAULT_HANDLERS defines canonical event types with proper payload shapes. Lint and type-check pass. The 2 failing tests in outbox-service-full.spec.ts and outbox.service.spec.ts are pre-existing timeout issues in retry/backoff logic unrelated to this fan-out registry story.
